trial_public_title: A prospective, Single-Centre study to investigate the plaque characteristics of lesions in young Indian patients of â‰¤40 years
trial_scientific_title_: A prospective, Single-Centre study to investigate the plaque characteristics in the culprit vessels in young Indian patients (â‰¥18 - â‰¤40 yrs) with ACS undergoing PCI by Optical Coherence Tomography imaging.
trial_indication: Other forms of acute ischemic heart disease,
trial_description: Acute coronary syndrome (ACS) is characterized by rapid stenosis or occlusion of the lumen caused by coronary thrombus formation, resulting in myocardial ischemia or necrosis. It is classified into ST-elevation acute myocardial infarction (STEMI), non-ST-elevation acute myocardial infarction (NSTEMI), and unstable angina (UA). Coronary plaques in ACS are greatly different from those in stable coronary artery disease (SCAD), being characterized by various morphological features and thrombus formation within the coronary artery. Culprit lesions of ACS can be identified as occluded or severely stenotic lesions on a coronary angiogram, but non-culprit lesions that are angiographically shown to be mildly or moderately stenotic also often represent lipid or vulnerable plaques with a necrotic core, suggesting that they are likely to lead to thrombus formation. Recent advances in intravascular imaging techniques such as intravascular ultrasound (IVUS) and optical coherence tomography (OCT) have made it possible to assess coronary lesions in greater detail. OCT is an imaging technique that employs near-infrared light with a wavelength of about 1300 nm emitted from the tip and visualizes the vascular lumen based on the light reflected from various parts of the lumen. Because of its high resolution (10 to 15 Î¼m), optical coherence tomography (OCT) has played an important role in elucidating the pathology of ACS by, for example, identifying thin-cap fibroatheroma (TCFA) with a large necrotic lipid core, one of the major causes of ACS, and its rupture, and characterizing coronary thrombi as red, white, or mixed thrombi based on morphology and the degree of signal attenuation This study will evaluate the plaque characteristics (minimum lumen area, fibrous cap thickness, lipid arc extension, presence of microcalcification macrophages infiltration and microchannel formation at the explored plaques) of culprit lesions at baseline. This information will be correlated with the follow clinical data in these patients undergoing PCI for ACS in young Indian patients.

trial_description: Novel oral anticoagulants are used to prevent thrombosis in several cardiac contexts such as atrial fibrillation, deep vein thrombosis etc. FDA in 2018, approved rivaroxaban for reduction of major adverse cardiovascular events in patients with chronic coronary artery disease (CAD) or peripheral arterial disease (PAD). The COMPASS published convincing evidence that supports the use of rivaroxaban along with aspirin in patients with stable CAD. trial_scientific_title_: A Double Blind, Multi-Arm, Clinical Study to Assess the Safety and Efficacy of a Gut Health Regimen for the Management of Constipation in subjects with Mild to Severe Constipation and Irritable Bowel Syndrome with Constipation (IBS-C).
trial_indication: :K581||Irritable bowel syndrome with constipation. Ayurveda Condition: VIBANDHA,
trial_description: This clinical study is a double-blind, multi-arm investigation aimed at evaluating the safety and efficacy of a gut health regimen for managing constipation in individuals diagnosed with mild to severe functional constipation or irritable bowel syndrome with constipation (IBS-C). Forty subjects aged 18â€“60, meeting specific inclusion criteria, will be enrolled. They will receive symptom-specific products targeting gut health, including probiotics, digestive aids, and stress-relief . The primary objective is to assess the regimenâ€™s effectiveness in reducing constipation symptoms over 30 days, with a possible extension to 56 days if necessary. Secondary objectives include monitoring changes in bowel movement frequency, stool consistency (using the Bristol Stool Chart), and participant-reported improvements in digestive health and relief from constipation. The study will also track adverse events,if any . Outcomes will be measured through baseline and weekly assessments, using scales like the VAS for symptom severity and the PAC-SYM questionnaire for subjective symptom relief.

trial_co_investigators: Ambanna Gowda, Sunita Kapoor, Dipti Gupta, Harsh Mittal
trial_description: The World Health Organization classifies anemia as decrease in the number red blood cells or insufficient oxygen carrying capacity to meet the physiological needs, which varies by age, sex, altitude, smoking and pregnancy status. Deficiency in folate, vitamin-B12, vitamin-A, chronic inflammations, ulcers, pregnancy, parasitic infections, hemoglobinopathies, etc. can all lead to anemia, but iron-deficiency anemia is considered to be one of the leading causes of anemia in the world. It is advisable for hemoglobin (Hb) values to be continuously measured among people who undergo hemodialysis or suspected with anemia and oligocythemia (Kavsaoglu, Polat, & Hariharan, 2015). The purpose of this study to validate the clinical performance of Vivaray Hb pro and use the device generated hemoglobin value for validation by comparing it with the hemoglobin value generated from the gold standard (calibrated blood cell counter).

trial_public_title: Assessment of cardiovascular disease related distress and psychosocial determinants: A prospective observational study
trial_scientific_title_: Assessment of cardiovascular disease related distress and psychosocial determinants: A prospective observational study
trial_indication: Subsequent ST elevation (STEMI) myocardial infarction of anterior wall,
trial_description: Cardiovascular disease (CVD) is a major health concern globally and in India contributing significantly to morbidity and mortality. While medical treatments and surgical interventions have advanced the psychosocial aspects of CVD such as patient distress and its impact on outcomes are less understood and thus often neglected. This study aims to address this gap by systematically evaluating the distress experienced by CVD patients. Understanding these relationships is crucial for developing holistic care strategies that encompasses both physical and psychological impacts ultimately aiming to improve patient management, enhance the quality of life and reduce the burden of CVD related distress.

trial_description: The Edwards SAPIEN 3 Transcatheter Heart Valve is indicated for use in patients with severe, symptomatic, calcified aortic stenosis who are considered at any risk for surgical valve replacement. In this study, we will see the successive model, S3, which developed with the intent to improve procedural outcomes in patients coming under all risk categories. The Edwards SAPIEN valve was the first available transcatheter valve to be used commercially. Several data from randomized clinical trials and large-scale registries have established this novel technique as a valid alternative for patients with severe Aortic Stenosis (AS) who are either inoperable or are at any risk for surgery. This technology initiated a paradigm shift in the way patients are being treated worldwide and further changed the field of interventional cardiology from focusing mainly on coronary disease to structural heart diseases arena. . Transcatheter heart valve therapy is available for commercial use for more than four years in India, but there is a need to examine long-term outcomes in Indian patients. The clinical registry is aimed to establish safety and efficacy evidence for SAPIEN 3 in the Indian patient population. This will be a prospective, single-arm, multi-center study to assess the safety and effectiveness of the To evaluate long term safety and efficacy of SAPIEN 3 transcatheter aortic valve implantation (TAVI) in Indian patients with severe symptomatic calcific aortic stenosis. The primary and secondary endpoints will be reported based on the As Treated (AT) Population. Descriptive and Inferential statistical analysis will be performed on all endpoints. Patient listings for adverse events, deaths, and reinterventions will include all enrolled patients. All clinically relevant baseline and follow-up variables will be tabulated. trial_public_title: Remote Monitoring of patients having implantable cardiac devices.
trial_scientific_title_: Remote Monitoring Evaluation with cardiac implantable devices in India
trial_sponsors: Max Super Speciality Hospital A Unit of Devki Devi Foundation
trial_co_investigators: Rajesh Saxena, AM Karthigesan, PK Sahoo, Sameer Mehrotra, John Roshan Jacob, Subhash Chandra, JS Makkar, Anil Saxena, Tapan Ghose, Shashidhar Pal, Nikhil Kumar, Praveen SV, Ajay Naik, Naveen Bhamri, Balbir Singh, Jaishankar k, Arun Gopi, Kapil Kumawat, Niraj Yadav
trial_description: Heart diseases have become one of the leading causes of human fatalities around the world; for instance, approximately 2.8 million people die each year of coronary heart disease and ischemic stroke. The treatment of such chronic diseases requires continuous and long-term monitoring to control threat. Implantable cardioverter-defibrillators (ICD) and cardiac resynchronization therapy (CRT-D) are effective therapies for patients with heart failure. With evolution of device and communication technology, device checks that were done manually in-ofï¬ce can now be fully performed accurately by the device itself at the patientâ€™s home. The data are sent automatically to the physician by wireless communication to a home transmitter and then to the physician, without requiring any intervention by the patient. Thus, remote follow-up may safely replace in-ofï¬ce visits, as long as no changes in programming are required. Another aspect is remote monitoring, which involves automatic transmissions of predeï¬ned alerts, such as onset of atrial ï¬brillation or abnormal lead impedance 1.Remote monitoring also provided early detection of heart failure events and reduce the number of urgent in-office visits and total health care use in patients with ICD or cardiac resynchronization systems in diverse clinical studies2. In general, different trials have shown multiple advantages of Remote monitoring , RM decreases the time to make a clinical decision, thereby preventing several worsening conditions, allowing rapid detection of arrhythmias, reducing inappropriate shocks and sparing ICD batteries or device malfunctioning Over the last few years, prospective trials have compared in-clinic and remote follow-up not only from a clinical standpoint, but also in terms of resource consumption3. Remote monitoring is now universally available for all cardiac implantable electronic devices (CIEDs), and it has become the standard of care for the management of patients with these devices. In many countries, it is being proven that patients with implantable defibrillator (ICD)or cardiac resynchronizing therapy (CRT) can benefit from remote monitoring (RM) both economically and clinically4-6 . However, now remote monitoring is a class I recommendation across several indications. In spite of that there is lower penetration of RM in India. The HRS Expert Consensus Statement on remote interrogation and monitoring for cardiovascular implantable electronic devices (CIEDs) recommends that remote monitoring and interrogation is recommended over a calendar-based schedule of in-person CIED evaluation alone (whenever feasible)7

trial_public_title: To study the impact of programming CRT devices with SyncAV on (LV reverse remodeling) compared with devices programmed to conventional CRT settings.
trial_scientific_title_: SyncAV Post Market Trial
trial_countries: India, United States of America, Canada, Malaysia, Republic of Korea, Singapore
trial_intervention_name: For those subjects randomized to the fixed AV delay arm, sites will measure QRS duration with the subjects intrinsic rhythm (pacing OFF) and at BiV pacing nominal settings. Sites will then program the subjects device using BiV pacing nominal settings. For those subjects randomized to SyncAV programmed ON, sites will measure QRS duration with the subjects intrinsic rhythm (pacing OFF), at BiV pacing nominal settings, with various SyncAV programmed offsets, and at LV first 30 ms, RV first 30 ms and LV-only pacing with optimal SyncAV offsets. The site will then program the subjects device using the SyncAV offset that provided the narrowest QRS duration.
trial_co_investigators: Sumit Anand, Girish Navasundi, Calambur Narasimhan, Ajay Naik , Balbir Singh, Kartikeya Bhargava, Kapil Kumawat
trial_description: The SyncAV Post-Market Trial is designed as a prospective, randomized, multi-center trial. The trial will require physicians to implant an Abbott CRT device and Abbott Quadripolar LV lead with any available right atrial and right ventricular leads. Sites will randomize subjects in a 1:1 ratio within 2 - 6 weeks post successful CRT implant: Arm 1 - SyncAV CRT programmed ON; Arm 2 - programmed fixed atrioventricular (AV) delay. For those subjects randomized to SyncAV programmed ON, sites will measure QRS duration with the subject’s intrinsic rhythm (pacing OFF), at biventricular (BiV) pacing nominal settings, with various SyncAV programmed offsets, and at LV first 30 ms, RV first 30 ms and LV-only pacing with optimal SyncAV offsets. The site will then program the subject’s device using the SyncAV offset that provided the narrowest QRS duration. For those subjects randomized to the fixed AV delay arm, sites will measure QRS duration with the subject’s intrinsic rhythm (pacing OFF) and at BiV pacing nominal settings. Sites will then program the subject’s device using BiV pacing nominal settings. Sites will collect data at baseline (before CRT implant), randomization, and at 3-month, 6-month, and 12-month visits. For subjects randomized to the SyncAV ON arm, sites will optimize the SyncAV feature again at 3 months and 6 months in the same manner as the randomization visit.

trial_public_title: Dozee device validation for vitals monitoring
trial_scientific_title_: Validation of the Dozee System
trial_indication: Validation of vitals including HR, HRV, RR, temperature, SpO2, BP, heart rhythm, posture, motion (movement accuracy and bed exit accuracy)
trial_description: The most common parameters supervised by healthcare professionals to monitor patientsâ€™ health are heart rate (HR), heart rate variability (HRV), respiration rate (RR), oxygen saturation (SpO2), temperature, blood pressure (BP), and heart rhythm [1]. Existing gold standard methods for measuring HR, HRV and RR necessitate the use of multi-lead ECG sensors [2] and nasal thermistor/nasal cannulas [3], which are both cumbersome and require a high level of patient compliance. These methods, too, necessitate some clinical expertise for proper application. Many advances have been made in the use of wearables to achieve long-term monitoring of vitals [4,5]. However wearables also require a high level of patient compliance. Monitoring of these vitals with less cumbersome techniques would be ideal in long term monitoring of patients and healthy people to detect any sudden abnormalities and long-term health deterioration. The Dozee device developed by Turtle Shell Technologies Pvt. Ltd., is a non-contact continuous vital parameters monitoring system. It is designed for continuous and contactless measurement of HR HR, RR, and SpO2 (when connected with an accessory). The device uses the concept of ballistocardiography and deploys piezoelectric sensors to capture the micro-vibrations originating from cardiac contractions, upper respiratory tract activity and body movements. This data is then analyzed by a proprietary algorithm and the values of the vital parameters are generated. The sensor sheet is placed below the mattress and captures data while the user is resting or sleeping and does not require a sensor to be fixed to the body making it ideal when compared to current methods. Monitoring peopleâ€™s vital signs outside an ICU often relies on nursing staff conducting checks at set intervals. The device can reduce the workload for nursing staff in non-ICU wards. This device is intended to be used to monitor individuals/patients greater than eighteen years of age (between 40kg-150kgs) and can measure their vital signs during sleep and resting state. The data can be viewed on the dashboard, exported, and even be directly transferred to the in-house hospital management software. trial_scientific_title_: Association between invasively measured central aortic pressure in patients undergoing coronary angiography and left ventricular diastolic function by echocardiography
trial_indication: I200||Unstable angina, (2) ICD-10 Condition: I24||Other acute ischemic heart diseases,
trial_description: Left ventricular diastolic dysfunction can be seen both in general population with poor clinical outcome and also in patients with certain cardiac conditions.Since the prevelence and clinical significance of heart failure with preserved ejection fraction has increased it is important to give more importance on LV diastolic function.Many studies has suggested that increased pulse pressure and LV afterload by stiffened aorta contributes to the development of LV diastolic dysfunction.The analysis of aortic pressure waveforms provides information on aortic and compliance.Due to the invasive nature of cardiac catheterization,there is limited information obtained on hemodynamic interaction between the left ventricle and aorta using invasive pressure monitoring,Thus, our study helps to investigate whether the parameters of invasively measured central aortic pressure waves are related with those of LV diastolic function and also to confirm the interaction between LV diastolic function and central aortic stiffness.The systolic BP,Diastolic BP,Mean BP,Pulse pressure,Fractional pulse pressure,Pulsatility index are the parameters used to assess aortic pressure and the echocardiographic parameters like chamber quantification of LA/LV,Dopler across mitral valve,TDI on IVS and lateral wall annulus and LV twist and untwist rate are used to assess LV diastolioc function.Our study helps to prove whether increased pulse pressure,Fractional pulse pressure and pulsatility index of ascending aorta measured by invasive catheterization were independently associated with reduced LV relaxation(eâ€™) and increased LV filling pressure(E/eâ€™).This finding supports a close interaction between aortic stiffness and LV diastolic dysfunction

trial_public_title: Testing Dozee™s Contactless Vital Sign Measurements
trial_scientific_title_: Clinical Validation of Dozee™s Contactless Vital Sign Measurements
trial_description: The most common parameters supervised by healthcare professionals to monitor patientsâ€™ health are heart rate (HR), respiration rate (RR), blood pressure (BP), oxygen saturation (SpO2), and temperature1 Conventional techniques for continuous monitoring of HR and RR rely on sophisticated equipment like multi-lead ECG sensors2 and nasal thermistors or nasal cannulas3. Continuous BP measurement typically involves intra-arterial BP monitoring (ABP), where a catheter is inserted into the artery for continuous BP monitoring4. However, ABP procedures are invasive and pose risks such as infection, bleeding, and thrombosis5. They are mainly restricted to Intensive Care Units (ICU) and are usually limited to critically ill patients, sometimes discontinued within a week due to discomfort and complications. For other hospitalized patients, periodic non-invasive cuff-based BP (NiBP) measurements are common, often done once a day. However, obtaining these readings can be difficult, disruptive (especially during sleep), and unreliable6,7. These traditional methods for HR, RR, and BP measurements are cumbersome and necessitate a high level of patient cooperation, as well as clinical expertise for accurate implementation. Turtle Shell Technologies Pvt. Ltd. â€™s (TST) Dozee system is a non-invasive continuous vitals monitoring system. The device employs piezoelectric sensors to capture micro-vibrations caused by cardiac contractions, upper respiratory tract activity, and body movements. It is based on the concept of ballistocardiography (BCG). The Dozee system is made up of non-contact sensor sheet(s) whose signals are used to calculate HR, RR, and non-contact blood pressure (NcBP). To measure temperature, SpO2, and NiBP, contact accessories such as pulse oximeter, temperature probe, and cuff BP respectively have been integrated. This is a single-arm, unblinded, prospective, observational non-randomized study. The study will include 400 +ICU patients who provide consent. This study is considered an observational study as no Dozee data will be shared with clinical staff or the subject that may influence care. The study duration of enrolment for each patient will be 24hrs-72hrs. Dias D, Paulo Silva Cunha J. Wearable Health Devices-Vital Sign Monitoring, Systems and Technologies. trial_public_title: Evaluation of NT-ProBNP marker to predict the cardiovascular risk in individuals with type 2 diabetes.
trial_scientific_title_: NT-ProBNP: a novel predictor of cardiovascular risk in type 2 diabetes
trial_indication: E118||Type 2 diabetes mellitus with unspecified complications,
trial_description: NT-proBNP (N-terminal pro-B-type natriuretic peptide) is a biomarker that has significant medical value in the assessment and management of cardiovascular diseases. The measurement of NT-proBNP levels in the blood provides valuable information for the risk, diagnosis, prognosis, and monitoring of various cardiovascular conditions. NT-proBNP levels have been found to provide valuable prognostic information in patients with acute coronary syndrome (ACS) and a risk predictor marker for CVD in type 2 diabetes patients. Elevated levels of NT-proBNP in CVD patients are associated with an increased risk of adverse cardiovascular events, including mortality. The measurement of NT-proBNP can help risk-stratify patients with heart failure and guide for superlative treatment decisions. The study aims to determine the NT-Pro BNP levels in type 2 diabetes patients with and without CVD in comparison to healthy controls in order to assess the risk of marker in CVD. The study also assess the differences between the three groups by evaluating the risk stratification of CVD in each group using a standard CVD risk tool. The outcome measures include the levels of NT-proBNP to find the risk of CVD. Also, the NT-ProBNP levels will be correlated with other markers such as fasting glucose, HbA1c, HDL, triglycerides, total cholesterol, LDL, micro-albuminuria, and creatinine to find their association. Risk stratification assessment for CVD using risk tools is also included in order to compare the risk analysis between the groups. Elevated levels of NT-proBNP in type 2 diabetes are associated with an increased risk of cardiovascular disease. The measurement of NT-proBNP can help risk-stratify patients with heart failure and guide for superlative treatment decisions.

trial_public_title: Comparison of  Recovery of  Functions After Stroke in Patients With Diabetes Mellitus and Without Diabetes Mellitus
trial_scientific_title_: Functional Recovery After Stroke In Patients With and Without Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus
trial_description: Diabetes is a group of metabolic diseases characterized by hyperglycemia resulting from defects in insulin secretion, insulin action, or both. There are more than 463 million cases of diabetes mellitus worldwide, of which at least 77 million are from India. The total prevalence of diabetes in India was 10.4 % in 2019. It was estimated that half of all patients with diabetes mellitus are not aware of their disease and are prone to develop complications. Diabetes mellitus poses a fourfold increased risk for cardiovascular disease including stroke. Neurological disorders including cerebrovascular disease were the leading cause of disability adjusted life years (DALYs) and the second most common cause of death worldwide. Hyperglycemia and insulin resistance increase the production of reactive oxygen species (ROS) leading to endothelial dysfunction and promote foam cell formation this accelerates atherosclerosis. Neuroimaging studies showed that patients with Type 2-diabetes mellitus had narrower lenticulostriate artery with decreased branching this may lead to greater neurological deficit and delayed recovery after stroke. Brain derived neurotropic factor (BDNF) which has a role in neuroplasticity was found to be low in stroke patients with diabetes and this was associated with slower recovery from stroke. Published literature from India comparing functional recovery after stroke in patients with and without diabetes is scanty, hence this study aims to determine whether presence of diabetes mellitus influences the functional recovery after stroke.

trial_public_title: The aim is to evaluate the safety and efficacy of MeRes100â„¢ Sirolimus Eluting BioResorbable Vascular Scaffold in coronary artery disease patients.It enhances coronary luminal diameter in symptomatic ischemic heart disease, addressing de novo lesions in native coronary arteries.
trial_scientific_title_: A prospective, single-arm, multi-centre study to evaluate the safety and performance of MeRes100â„¢ Sirolimus Eluting BioResorbable Vascular Scaffold System by clinical and imaging outcomes in patients with coronary artery disease
trial_intervention_name: This is a prospective study aims to assess the safety and effectiveness of the MeRes100â„¢ Sirolimus Eluting BioResorbable Vascular Scaffold System in at least 200 eligible patients undergoing PTCA and scaffolding procedures, with the study continuing for up to 3 years. MeRes100â„¢ Sirolimus Eluting BioResorbable Vascular Scaffold System consists of a balloon expandable scaffold made from polymer poly-L-lactide (PLLA) which is a biocompatible and bioresorbable polymer which undergoes hydrolytic degradation in the body and eventually gets eliminated as carbon dioxide and water. The active component is therapeutic agent, an antiproliferative drug viz. Sirolimus. The drug is formulated with biocompatible bioabsorbable polymer viz. poly-DL-lactide (PDLLA) which acts as drug reservoir and controls drug release rate. The scaffold delivery system is a percutaneous transluminal coronary angioplasty (PTCA) catheter. The MeRes100â„¢ BRS is designed to enhance the diameter of coronary arteries in individuals experiencing symptomatic ischemic heart disease caused by de novo lesion in their native coronary arteries with a procedure time limit of up to 2 hours.
trial_indication: Atherosclerotic heart disease of native coronary artery,
trial_co_investigators: Rajesh T, Praveen Sreekumar, Anil Kumar, Tahsin Neduvanchery, Deepak Davidson, Shafeeq Mattummal, Suresh Davis, Mahesh Nalin Kumar
trial_description: This is a prospective, single-arm, multi-centre study for evaluating the safety and performance of of the MeRes100â„¢ Sirolimus Eluting BioResorbable Vascular Scaffold System in treating coronary artery disease. It involves 200 patients across multiple centers, evaluating both clinical and imaging results. The MeRes100â„¢ BRS is intended to enhance the diameter of coronary arteries in individuals with symptomatic ischemic heart disease cause due to de novo lesion in their native coronary arteries, specifically for those eligible for PTCA and scaffolding procedures.

trial_public_title: OCT findings in heart attack patients with apprently normal/near normal angiogram
trial_scientific_title_: Optical coherence Tomography (OCT) findings in patients of myocardial infarction with non obstructed coronary arteries
trial_intervention_name: Optical coherence Tomography findings in heart attack patients with apparently normal/near normal angiogram
trial_description: Myocardial infarction with non obstructive coronary arteries (MINOCA) is a syndrome with different causes, characterised by clinical evidence of acute myocardial infarction (AMI) with normal or near normal coronary arteries on angiography (stenosis severity < 50%). Incidence of the same has been reported to be varying from 6-14% in MI with obstructive CAD. Intravascular optical coherence tomography (OCT) is a high resolution imaging technique that is increasingly being used in interventional cardiology for the investigation of coronary artery disease (CAD). OCT may be helpful for identifying the culprit lesion, or for ruling out other mechanisms such as coronary dissection or in situ thrombosis, which may not have been correctly appreciated during angiography. Few imaging studies earlier have reported coronary artery pathologies like plaque rupture, plaque erosion, calcium nodule disruption and intimal dissection in 60-80% cases. OCT studies have also resulted in change in treatment strategies like stenting, which have potential prognostic implications. However, there is no systemic Indian data is this regard. Our aim is to study the OCT features of MINOCA and describe the incidence of various pathological findings in this patient subset in our centre.

trial_public_title: Evaluation of a Leadless Pacemaker System
trial_scientific_title_: Chronic Evaluation of Safety & Efficacy of a Leadless Pacemaker System in Subjects With Class I Or II Indication for Pacemaker
trial_sponsors: CALYAN CARDIAC THERAPEUTICS INDIA PVT LTD
trial_intervention_name: The Calyan leadless pacemaker system is an investigational device for the treatment of cardiac disorders requiring ventricular pacing. The principal components of the Calyan pacemaker system are an implantable pulse generator, a delivery system, and an external programming system. Patients enrolled in this study will be implanted with the leadless pacemaker
trial_description: This is an open-label feasibility study. Patients with a class I or II indication for implantation of a single-chamber ventricular pacemaker according to ACC/AHA/HRS guidelines will be implanted with a leadless pacemaker device. The device will be implanted underneath the ribcage and anchored to the xiphoid process, with electrodes placed on the cardiac epicardial surface. Chronic VVI pacing will be delivered for 3 months, with data collection at 1 week, 2 weeks, 1 month, and 3 months. Safety will be determined by the event-free rate, after pacemaker implantation, from all systemrelated and implantation-related serious adverse events (SAEs) at 3 months postimplant. Efficacy will be determined by the number of patients with successful chronic ventricular pacing, including successful pacemaker implantation chronic capture of the ventricle.

trial_intervention_name: Type 	Name 	Details Intervention 	Ayurveda (SHARP veda) 	2 tablets per day for 6 months. Intervention 	Cetrizinc SR 	1 tablet per day for 6 months
trial_indication: I708||Atherosclerosis of other arteries. Ayurveda Condition: DHAMANIPRATICAYAHÂ (KEVALA-KAPHA),
trial_description: Calcium is the most abundant mineral in the human body. Although most of the calcium is found in teeth and bone, approximately 1% is dissolved in the bloodstream. As the human body ages, calcium can deposit in various parts of the body. Arterial calcium development is closely related to vascular injury, inflammation, and repair. Calcification occurs very early in the process of atherosclerosis. Atherosclerosis is a common condition that develops when a sticky substance called plaque builds up inside your arteries. Atherosclerosis develops slowly as cholesterol, fat, blood cells and other substances in your blood form plaque. When the plaque builds up, it causes your arteries to narrow. This reduces the supply of oxygen-rich blood to tissues of vital organs in the body. Atherosclerosis is the result of hyperlipidaemia and lipid oxidation and has always been a major cause of mortality in developed countries. Coronary artery calcification was previously thought to be a benign process, and the calcified lesion increases in accordance with ageing. Medial calcification is associated with arterial stiffness, which increases risk for adverse cardiovascular events. Therefore, SHARP-veda (Sanjeevaniâ€™s Holistic Atherosclerosis Reversal Program) therapy is an integrated treatment method which includes 3 phases of the treatment stage namely: Phase 1(Anti-inflammatory and arterial chelation) Phase 2 (Anti-oxidant), Phase3 (Arterial de-stiffening). In every phase of treatment, the intervention will consist of all 3 groups which will include - Aahar (Diet modifications), Vihar (Lifestyle modification like sleep,yoga,exercise), Aushadi (medications).

trial_description: Name of Sponsor/Company: Torrent Pharmaceuticals Ltd, India Name of Study Drug: Dapagliflozin & Sitagliptin combination Title of Study: Post Approval Concurrent, Observational Study to Assess Safety, Clinical Utilization and Effectiveness of SItagliptin & DApagliflozin Combination Therapy in the treatment of Type 2 Diabetes for EXtra Glycemic Advantages (SIDAXA Study) EudraCT no.: Not applicable Indication: Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus Objectives: To assess the role and positioning of Dapagliflozin and Sitagliptin combination in the management of T2DM cases conducted at real world outpatient settings of India. The combination will act on complementary mechanisms to correct Metabolic traits in Indian diabetics while avoiding the twin issues of gluco- and lipotoxicity due to â€˜compensatoryâ€™ lipolysis and hyperglucagonemia Study population: Type 2 diabetes Study design: Post-approval, concurrent, observational, single-arm, open label study. Study endpoints: Primary Endpoint: Â§ Mean changes from baseline in glycated hemoglobin (HbA1c) level at 12 W Â§ FPG and PPBG at 4 W Secondary Endpoints: Â§ Proportion of participants with treatment emergent adverse event during observation period Â§ Change for HbA1c in T2d cases with CAD at 12 W Â§ Change in Albumin & LDL-C at 12 W Â§ Change in FPG and PPBG at 4 W Â§ Change in SGPT and SGOT at 12 W Â§ Change in BP at 4W and 12W Â§ Change in Weight at 4W and 12W Number of patients (planned): 18000 patients will be enrolled in the study from 1800 different sites across India. Main criteria for inclusion: Ã¼ Male or female patients 18 to 59 years Ã¼ Known T2DM cases with uncontrolled HbA1c >7 % requiring Sitagliptin and Dapagliflozin combination on top of existing therapy Ã¼ T2DM cases with one or more additional risk factors or established CAD that is stable for >3 months following hospitalization Ã¼ Patients willing follow-up for 12 W at the same centre Main criteria for exclusion: Ã¼ Hypersensitivity to SGLT2i or Metformin Extended release formulation Ã¼ Unstable or untreated CAD with HF not receiving either ARNI or Diuretics Ã¼ CKD with eGFR <45 ml/min Ã¼ Hepatic enzyme elevation X3 ULN Ã¼ DKA and/or history or prior use of Dapagliflozin or Canagliflozin or Empagliflozin Ã¼ For women only - currently pregnant (confirmed with positive pregnancy test) or breastfeeding Ã¼ Type 1 diabetes mellitus patients Ã¼ Sexually active woman of childbearing age not practicing accepted birth control method Ã¼ Urinary tract infection or acute pyelonephritis in the 30 days prior to study entry Duration of study participation: 12 months (Screening + Observation) Duration of observation: 12 W Investigational product, dosage and mode of administration: Sitagliptin and Dapagliflozin is indicated in adults aged 18 years and older with type 2 diabetes mellitus to improve glycaemic control as: Ã¼ It is indicated for the treatment of patients with Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus inadequately controlled on Metformin monotherapy. Start with Sitagliptin 100mg & Dapagliflozin 5mg escalate to Sitagliptin 100 mg & Dapagliflozin 10 mg combination, the recommended dose is Sitagliptin 100mg & Dapagliflozin 10 mg given once daily as initial-line or add-on therapy with metformin. As observed with other SGLT2 inhibitors, the cases requiring consistent Insulin therapy will be omitted from the final study analyses

trial_public_title: A clinical trial to study the effects of two diet interventions, Calorie Restricted Diet and Time Restricted Intermittent Fasting in Individuals with type 2 diabetes mellitus
trial_scientific_title_: Effect of Continuous Calorie Restricted Diet versus Time Restricted Intermittent Fasting Diet on the Glycemic Variability, Cardio-metabolic Markers and Quality of Life in Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus: A Randomized Crossover Clinical Trial
trial_intervention_name: Intervention 	Time Restricted Intermittent Fasting 	No calorie restriction but divided into only two meals a day (morning and evening) and fasting for rest of the time. The intervention will be followed for 3 months (12 weeks)
trial_description: Recent clinical data suggest that Glycemic Variability (GV), particularly Time in Range (%TIR), is associated with micro-vascular as well as macro-vascular complications in Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus (T2DM), independent of HbA1c levels. To date, studies on the importance of lowering the Glycemic Variability (GV)- an independent factor for diabetes complications, are present but studies on the effect of dietary intervention on GV are very scarce. Thus, we aimed to evaluate the effect of Continuous Calorie Restricted Diet (CCRD) (defined as reducing the daily caloric intake by 500 from the BMR of a person) versus Time-Restricted Intermittent Fasting Diet (TRIF) (popularized in India as two meals a day diet) on GV in individuals with Type 2 Diabetes (T2D). This study is a Randomized open-label cross-over clinical trial. The primary outcome of our study will be the GV data generated using the Continuous Glucose Monitoring System (CGMS) called Freestyle Libre Pro by Abbott, at the end of both the interventions. The secondary outcome of our study is to study the changes in the cardio metabolic parameters such as Fasting blood sugar, Postprandial blood sugar, HbA1c, Lipid Profile, and Postprandial Triglyceride after following the two interventions. we will also be looking into the adherence level and Diet-related Quality of Life (QoL) in these participants. This study will be the first one to ascertain the potency of CCR and TRIF on the GV among people with T2DM. The results acquired can form a structure for determining the course of action in helping the patients to improve their glycemic control, which can help to increase general awareness.

trial_public_title: The combination of Berberis aristata and Phyllanthus emblica extract in the management of prediabetes.
trial_scientific_title_: A Randomized, placebo-controlled study to assess the efficacy & safety of the combination of Berberis aristata and Phyllanthus emblica extract supplementation in prediabetic subjects.
trial_intervention_name: Intervention Group 1: Berberis aristata extract and Phyllanthus emblica extract 500 mg capsule (NextBeriâ„¢). Twice a day after breakfast and after dinner for 180 days. Intervention Group 2: Berberis aristata extract and Phyllanthus emblica extract 250 mg capsule (NextBeriâ„¢). Twice a day after breakfast and after dinner for 180 days Comparator Agent Placebo 500 mg capsule.
trial_description: Standardized extracts of Berberis aristata and Phyllanthus emblica contain constituents that can regulate blood sugar levels and aid in the management of prediabetes. The purpose of this study is to evaluate the safety and effectiveness of Berberis aristata and Phyllanthus emblica extract capsules in prediabetic individuals. By combining the current research and clinical trials on the pharmacological benefits of micronutrients, it is possible to address the critical medical concern of the expanding prediabetic population by enhancing glycemic control, halting the progression of the disease, and potentially reversing the condition. This study employs a randomized, controlled clinical methodology to evaluate the efficacy and safety of nutritional supplementation in individuals at risk of developing diabetes. There will be daily telephonic follow-up of subjects for the absence of major lifestyle changes that would impact the study outcomes. The clinical validation of the investigational product compared to placebo will offer diabetologists and caregivers evidence and guidance for the therapeutic usage of standardized nutrients and phytoconstituents in the management of prediabetes.

trial_description: Name of Sponsor/Company: Torrent Pharmaceuticals Ltd, India Name of Study Drug: Dapagliflozin + Sitagliptin FDC Title of Study: Post Approval Concurrent, ObServational Study to Assess Safety, ClinIcal UtiLization and Effectiveness of Sitagliptin & Dapagliflozin Fixed Dose Combination (FDC) in the treatment of Type 2 Diabetes with ASCVD (SAIL Study) EudraCT no.: Not applicable Indication: Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus Objectives: To assess the role and positioning of Dapagliflozin/Sitagliptin in the management of T2DM cases conducted at real world outpatient settings of India. The FDC will act on complementary mechanisms to correct Metabolic traits in Indian diabetics while avoiding the twin issues of gluco- and lipotoxicity due to â€˜compensatoryâ€™ lipolysis and hyperglucagonemia Study population: Type 2 diabetes Study design: Post-approval, concurrent, observational, single-arm, open label study. Study endpoints: Primary Endpoint: Â§ Mean changes from baseline in glycated hemoglobin (HbA1c) level at 12 W Secondary Endpoints: Â§ Proportion of participants with treatment emergent adverse event during observation period Â§ Change for HbA1c in T2D cases with ASCVD at 12 W Â§ Change in Albumin & LDL-C at 12 W Â§ Change in FPG and PPBG at 4 W Number of patients (planned): 30000 patients will be enrolled in the study from 1200 different sites across India. Main criteria for inclusion: Ã¼ Male or female patients 18 to 59 years Ã¼ Known T2DM cases with uncontrolled HbA1c >7 % requiring Dapagliflozin/Sitagliptin as fixed dose combination Ã¼ T2DM cases with one or more additional risk factors or ASCVD that is stable for >3 months following hospitalization Ã¼ Patients willing follow-up for 12 W at the same centre Main criteria for exclusion: Ã¼ Hypersensitivity to SGLT2i or Metformin Extended release formulation Ã¼ Unstable or untreated CAD with HF not receiving either ARNI or Diuretics Ã¼ CKD with eGFR <45 ml/min Ã¼ Hepatic enzyme elevation X3 ULN Ã¼ DKA and/or history or prior use of Dapagliflozin or Canagliflozin or Empagliflozin Ã¼ For women only - currently pregnant (confirmed with positive pregnancy test) or breast-feeding Ã¼ Type 1 diabetes mellitus patients Ã¼ Sexually active woman of childbearing age not practicing accepted birth control method Ã¼ Urinary tract infection or acute pyelonephritis in the 30 days prior to study entry Duration of study participation: 12 months (Screening + Observation) Duration of observation: 12 Weeks Investigational product, dosage and mode of administration: Sitagliptin + Dapagliflozin is indicated in adults aged 18 years and older with type 2 diabetes mellitus to improve glycemic control as: Ã¼ It is indicated for the treatment of patients with Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus inadequately controlled on Metformin monotherapy. The recommended dose is Sitagliptin 100 + Dapagliflozin 10 mg given once daily as initial-line or add-on therapy with metformin. As observed with other SGLT2 inhibitors, the cases requiring consistent Insulin therapy will be omitted from the final study analysis Criteria for evaluation: Effectiveness: Clinical response: The clinical response will be assessed for all patients who have received at least 12 W of treatment especially for the change in HbA1c to be assessed Safety: Adverse events: as per WHO classification, incidence of â€˜Commonâ€™ Treatment emergent adverse events of â‰¥ 1%. STATISTICAL METHODS: Data will be presented using descriptive statistics in the form of frequencies and percentages for qualitative variables, means, medians, standard deviations, minimal and maximal values and 95% confidence interval for quantitative variables. The continuous and categorical variables will be assessed for significance using the Studentâ€™s T and Fischerâ€™s Exact Test with p <0.05 considered to be clinically significant using the two-tailed test Primary efficacy analyses: Change in HbA1c will be analyzed in the T2DM cases enrolled for history of multiple risk factors (Dyslipidemia, Hypertension, Smoking and/or obesity with BMI >25) or ASCVD history for 12W observation period. Considering a treatment difference in HbA1c for 0.04% +/-0.7 % between the two groups, of cases with multiple risk factors and/or ASCVD with a dropout rate that is expected at the end of 12 W therapy, a sample size of 30000 cases had been planned for enrollment at 1200 centers across India. In case of SAE, the clinical investigator or the sponsor (representative) needs to report the Event information on pv@torrentpharma.com and the CDSCO Suspect ADR Report Form with details on the event along as Date of Awareness (DOA)

trial_description: Gestational diabetes mellitus (GDM) is a common disorder that affects approximately 5% of pregnant women and is associated with either neonatal (macrosomia, hypoglycemia, birth trauma) or maternal complications (pre-eclampsia, Caeserean-section). Additionally, women with a history of Gestational diabetes mellitus (GDM) have a substantially increased risk of developing type 2 Diabetes Mellitus (T2DM) later in life. Normal pregnancy induces insulin resistance through the diabetogenic effects of placental hormones and progesterone. Gestational diabetes mellitus (GDM) occurs when the reduction in insulin sensitivity is no longer balanced by an increase in insulin secretion. It has been suggested that Gestational Diabetes Mellitus (GDM) and Type 2 Diabetes are in principle the same metabolic disorder. GLP-1 (Glucagon like peptide) and Glucose-dependent insulinotropic polypeptide (GIP) are strong enhancers of glucose-stimulated insulin secretion. This incretin effect is reduced in Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus. We plan to evaluate whether an impaired secretion of GLP-1 (Glucagon like peptide) plays a significant role in the development of diabetes mellitus in pregnancy. Purpose of study: It will help us to identify pathophysiology of Gestational Diabetes Mellitus, which in itself is a risk factor for Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus. It will also help us to identify patients at risk of progression to Type 2 Diabetes and provide better screening and treatment options to prevent its onset.

trial_scientific_title_: A prospective, single-centre, single-arm registry to evaluate short and mid-term experience of Mozecâ„¢ SEB Sirolimus Eluting Rx PTCA Balloon Dilatation Catheter in patients with coronary artery disease
trial_intervention_name: NIL
trial_indication: Other forms of acute ischemic heart disease,
trial_description: Percutaneous coronary intervention (PCI) often with the implantation of a stent is considered standard revascularization treatment of flow-limiting coronary stenosis. The first introduced stent was a bare metal stent (BMS) to reduce restenosis of the treated segment by balloon angioplasty. However, restenosis from neointimal hyperplasia in the stented area, known as in-stent restenosis, remained a complication of BMS[17] . By combining the mechanical balloon dilatation of the vessel with local delivery of antiproliferative medicine, introduction of the drug-eluting stent (DES) reduced the incidence of instent restenosis. However, DESs imply other limitations, including increased bleeding risks associated with the need for prolonged dual antiplatelet therapy (DAPT) and the risk of late and very late stent thrombosis (ST)[9,10] . Therefore, current study aims to collect real-world data of Mozecâ„¢ SEB Sirolimus Eluting Rx PTCA Balloon Dilatation Catheter in patients with coronary artery disease.

trial_description: The PRECISION Trial is an observational study aimed at evaluating the predictive value of the SYNTAX Score II for assessing cardiovascular risk in patients undergoing percutaneous coronary intervention (PCI). The study will analyze retrospective data to predict major adverse cardiovascular events (MACE), including death, myocardial infarction, and repeat revascularization. Secondary outcomes include comparisons with other risk stratification tools, procedural success, long-term prognosis, and subgroup analyses by demographics and comorbidities. The expected sample size is 700 patients, with data collected from 2021â€“2024.

trial_indication: This study establishes normal echocardiographic values for healthy Indian adults, supporting accurate diagnosis and reducing cardiac misdiagnoses by addressing population-specific gaps.
trial_description: This retrospective observational study aims to establish normal echocardiographic reference values for healthy Indian adults using existing echocardiographic data. The lack of population-specific reference ranges often leads to misdiagnosis or inaccurate assessments in clinical practice. By analyzing data from a diverse cohort, the study will define normal ranges for key echocardiographic parameters, contributing to improved diagnostic accuracy and better clinical outcomes in cardiac care for the Indian population.

trial_intervention_name: Intervention Local Healthy Moderate Carbohydrate Diet and Defined Exercise Diet counselling and plan Calories- 20 calorie/kgIBW/day ,44-46 % carbohydrate, 25-30% proteins. 25-30 % fatsvegetarian and non-vegetarian meal plans Daily 40 minutes of walking with 10 minutes of deep breathing
trial_description: This prospective interventional randomized study will be carried out for a total duration of 18 months. Total 117 subjects who are on two or less oral antidiabetic agents will be enrolled from two centers. Participants with HbA1c of 6.5- 9% and fasting blood glucose of less than 200 mg/dL will be included in the study.They will be recruited as per the inclusion and exclusion criteria mentioned. Intervention in the form of Local Healthy Moderate Carbohydrate Diet (LHMC) with defined exercise will be given for a period of six months, during which all the oral antidibetic agents will be stopped ( intervention period). .They will be followed up six months and one year after the end of intervention. During the intervention period continuous glucose monitoring system will be applied at the start and at the end of the study to all the subjects. Laboratory investigations and screening for microvascular complications will be carried out at the start and at the end of intervention. All the subjects will be followed up every month and thorogh physical examination, fasting and post meal blood glucose estimation will be carried out in each visit. Dietary consultation will be done at every visit. HbA1c will be measured at the start, at three months and at the end of the study. Diet adherence will be checked weekly for each subject by asking for food photographs and unannounced phone calls for dietary recall history. Rescue medication will be added as per the protocol.

trial_public_title: To check the the risk of liver damage due to Diabetes in young patients of Diabetes both type 1 and type 2
trial_scientific_title_: Study Of Prevalence of Non-Alcoholic Fatty Liver Disease in young individuals with Type II and Type I Diabetes in Central India.
trial_indication: Fatty (change of) liver, not elsewhere classified,
trial_description: In order to explore the prevalence of non-alcoholic fatty liver disease (NAFLD) among people in Central India who have been diagnosed with type 1 and type II diabetes mellitus (T1DM & T2DM), the research team behind this project will use a prospective cohort design. Additionally, it will investigate the effectiveness of diagnostic approaches in detecting and monitoring NAFLD, as well as the association between NAFLD and a variety of clinical indicators, risk factors, and the influence of NAFLD on glycaemic control and diabetic complications. Individuals who have been diagnosed with type I and type II diabetes will be recruited from a variety of healthcare facilities and diabetic clinics located throughout Central India. Calculations of statistical power will be used to estimate the size of the sample that will be taken in order to guarantee an accurate representation of the population. Individuals who have a verified diagnosis of type 1 and type II diabetes and the capacity to offer informed consent will meet the inclusion criteria for this study. Individuals who have a history of excessive alcohol consumption, known liver illnesses other than NAFLD, and those who have comorbidities that may confound the study outcomes will be excluded from the study according to the exclusion criteria.

trial_public_title: To check how GlucoSEB affects the blood sugar level of diabetic and prediabetic patients after eating a meal.
trial_scientific_title_: A double blind, randomized, crossover, placebo-controlled study to determine effect of GlucoSEB on postprandial glucose level.
trial_intervention_name: GlucoSEB is a proprietary blend of enzymes, probiotics and herbal extract which together cause decline in sugar levels. Therefore, GlucoSEB would lower the glycemic response of food by preventing formation of free glucose from sucrose. The study involves consumption of 2 capsules 30 mins before meal (morning) orally with room temperature water for 04 days. The GlucoSEB capsules will be provided in bottles containing 60 capsules. So, the total duration of the intervention will be of 04 days.
trial_description: This is a double blind, randomized, crossover, placebo-controlled study. The study will be conducted at approximately 1-2 number of centers in India, having qualified Investigators. The duration of each subject is 16 days; Screening and Randomization (Day -2 and -1), the treatment (04 days) and End visit (Day 13). The study involves a total of 7 visits. In current study, we propose using combination of enzymes, herbal extract, and probiotics for treating hyperglycaemia to aid blood sugar management. GlucoSEB has been tested safe in a toxicology study; while herbal extracts generally considered safe for human consumption and has been reported for diabetes treatment. Additionally, the probiotic strains have proven record of safety and tolerability under different clinical trials. Thus, GlucoSEB would provide a complete solution for sugar management that comprised of meticulously selected enzymes, potent bioactive herbal extract/s and probiotics. Current clinical study designed to understand the biological impact of all these powerful natural aids in combating hyperglycaemia/ diabetes mellitus to support overall wellbeing. The study will be initiated only after the receipt of ethics committee (EC) approval and CTRI Registration.

trial_public_title: Clinical trial study to investigate the safety of SucroSEB and SEB TG which affects the metabolism and gut health of diabetic adults
trial_scientific_title_: A randomized, double blind, parallel, placebo-controlled study to evaluate metabolic health and gut microbiome in presence of SucroSEB and SEB TG and to determine their safety in diabetic adults
trial_intervention_name: Intervention 	SEB TG 	500mg, 2 capsules per meal (lunch and dinner), oral route of administration - swallow as a whole with water just before having meal, for 180 days Intervention 	SucroSEB 	500mg, 2 capsules per meal (lunch and dinner), oral route of administration - swallow as a whole with water just before having meal, for 180 days
trial_indication: E118||Type 2 diabetes mellitus with unspecified complications,
trial_description: In the most recent studies, the research conducted emphasizes the importance of focusing on the interplay between the gut microbiome and metabolic control, particularly with attention on how it mediates glucose metabolism. T2DM may also be seen as a consequence of gut dysfunction, termed as gut dysbiosis, due to the imbalanced structure and functional diversity of the gut microbiota. It has also been shown that dysbiosis inclines the body towards having an increased state of inflammation along with insulin resistance and also elevated levels of gut permeability which combine to deteriorate the metabolic processes. Hence, several studies have pointed to the gut microbiome also as an important target for treating T2DM and improving metabolic health. This has opened up the avenue for combinatorial therapies using enzymes and probiotics that could affect carbohydrate metabolism in addition to targeting gut health. Enzyme blends SEB TG and SucroSEB provide a unique combination of metabolic modulation and microbiome-oriented therapeutics that can be utilized as a metabolic therapy. SEB TG targets starch-like substances and produces slowly digestible oligosaccharides that can withstand rapid degradation within the intestine. These oligosaccharides, also promote the growth of intestinal bacteria thus exerting prebiotic effect. SucroSEB, on the other hand, hydrolyzes sucrose and would produce fibers; thus, may be able to blunt postprandial blood glucose responses and further aid the gut microbiota. These formulations aimed at reducing dysglycemia would enhance indicators of the metabolic syndrome and the normal function of the gut microbial structure. The SEB TG and SucroSEB are being evaluated under the consideration that these formulations may be included in the therapy of patients with diabetes for two main reasons: Improvement of Metabolic Parameters: The SEB TG and SucroSEB supplementation would reduce the glycemic index of the offending carbohydrates as these ingredients retard carbohydrate digestion and control discharge of free glucose into blood circulation. This dietary modification should help to lower post prandial glucose elevations, which would help protect against glucose toxicity, one of the main causes of insulin resistance. In addition, there is evidence supporting the production of prebiotic fibers from enzymatic action (under in-vitro conditions) that would enhance insulin sensitivity, normalizes hepatic glucose output, and decrease inflammation, all of which are essential for sustaining metabolic balance. Modulation of the Gut Microbiota: The emergence of links between gut dysbiosis and T2DM has consolidated the view that maintaining a healthy microbiome is essential for improving metabolic health. SEB TG and SucroSEB would promote the growth of good bacteria in the intestine by incorporating prebiotic fibers that help in balancing glucose metabolism and insulin sensitivity. Furthermore, these enzymes, by promoting a healthy gut microbiota, may lower systemic inflammation and promote gut barrier function thereby reducing the negative impact of hyperglycemia. Given their role in regulating metabolism plus the microbiome health, these enzymes can serve as a new treatment option for patients suffering from T2DM. These enzymes not only tackle dysglycemia but also the causative dysbiosis, which renders them ideal for diabetes management. The current clinical trial seeks to evaluate the biological functions of SEB TG and SucroSEB while investigating their capacities to alter the gut microflora, enhance glucose metabolism and reinforce health and wellness in general. In this dual approach SEB TG and SucroSEB create new opportunities to enhance metabolic effectiveness and prevent long-term diabetes related complications.

trial_public_title: A clinical trial to evaluate the safety of GlucoSEB PB and its impact on metabolism and gut health in adults with diabetes
trial_scientific_title_: A randomized, double blind, parallel, placebo-controlled study to evaluate metabolic health and gut microbiome in presence of GlucoSEB PB in diabetic adults as integrative care.
trial_intervention_name: Intervention 	GlucoSEB PB 	500mg, 2 capsules per meal (lunch and dinner), oral route of administration, swallow as a whole with water just before having meal, for 180 days
trial_indication: E118||Type 2 diabetes mellitus with unspecified complications,
trial_description: T2DM can be a long-term condition that requires extended blood sugar management: the combined approaches such as pharmacological, physical and, in recent years, therapeutic interventions directed at the microbiome are the most effective in managing it (Diagnosis and Classification of Diabetes Mellitus, 2013). While standard formulations which include insulin, metformin and SGLT2 inhibitors assist patients to manage their blood glucose levels, they rarely come without a fair share of side effects such as hypoglycemia, weight gain or gastrointestinal problems (Wilcox et al., 2020). They do not, however, resolve the underlying problems that lead to insulin resistance and Î²-cell dysfunction. For this reason, there has been a growing need to look for new and safer, and combined therapies that help not only control hyperglycemia but also treat underlying conditions, the imbalanced metabolic state (Cerf, 2013).The enzyme blend GlucoSEB PB can be a part of diabetic care regimen. The enzymes modulate carbohydrate metabolism while probiotics support in improving gut health. Numerous studies have reported that the gut microflora is closely related to body metabolism. An imbalance of intestinal microbiota, or dysbiosis, is one characteristic of T2DM that leads to metabolic dysregulation by causing insulin resistance, systemic inflammation and increased gut permeability (Hou et al., 2022; Wu et al., 2023; Xia et al., 2021). GlucoSEB PB consists of components that have gut microbiome targeted mechanisms primarily focused on enhancing metabolic parameters in patients with diabetes rather than solely targeting dysglycemia. The aim of evaluating GlucoSEB PB is described as follows: ï‚· Metabolic Health: The enzyme blend is useful in decreasing the glycemic index of consumed carbohydrates by controlling the free glucose into the bloodstream, thus lowering blood glucose levels after a meal. This might have special significance in avoiding glucose toxicity which is one of the factors that lead to insulin resistance. The manufacture of prebiotic fibers also helps in metabolic health by altering the gut microbiota which may increase insulin sensitivity and decrease systemic inflammation levels. ï‚· Gut Microbiome Modulation: Since there is a wealth of data associating gut dysbiosis and T2DM; promoting good gut bacteria through GlucoSEB PB is of particular interest. There are studies that support the use of Probiotics as adjunct therapy for blood sugar control and those that reduce inflammation and improve metabolic disease. Since GlucoSEB PB may promote a healthier microbiome, it may also have a wider effect on metabolic health other than dysglycemia. GlucoSEB PB treatment combines the benefits of targeting metabolic and gut health to diminish the ill effects caused due to diabetes. GlucoSEB PB is an advanced therapy that promises new avenues aimed at diabetes management.

trial_public_title: Study to Assess Effect of Remogliflozin Compared to Dapagliflozin on 24 Hour Glucose Profile Assessed by Continuous Glucose Monitoring in Indian Patients of Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus (remit- Gv)
trial_scientific_title_: A Prospective Multicenter Randomized Open Label, Active Controlled Study to Assess Effect of Remogliflozin Compared to Dapagliflozin on 24 Hour Glucose Profile Assessed By Continuous Glucose Monitoring in Indian Patients of Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus.
trial_intervention_name: Comparator Agent Dapagliflozin 10 mg Dapagliflozin 10mg tablet to be consumed by the T2DM patients once daily orally for period of 42 days. Intervention Remogliflozin 100mg 100 mg Remogliflozin Tablet twice daily to be consumed by the T2DM patients for 42 days.
trial_co_investigators: Pranjal Ausekar, Suhas Erande, K D Modi, K Srikanth
trial_description: A prospective multicenter randomized open-label, active-controlled study to assess the effect of Remogliflozin compared to Dapagliflozin on 24-hour glucose profile assessed by continuous glucose monitoring in Indian patients of type 2 diabetes mellitus is designed to evaluate the effect of Remogliflozin on 24-hour glucose profile assessed by parameters derived from continuous glucose monitoring. Also to compare the effect of Remogliflozin on 24-hour glucose levels (including post-prandial glucose levels) with Dapagliflozin assessed by parameters derived from continuous glucose monitoring in Type 2 DM patients. Along with these primary objectives secondary objective is to evaluate the safety and tolerability of Remogliflozin etabonate in comparison to Dapagliflozin. In this study patients will be randomized using a computer-generated randomization sequence into two treatment arms in the ratio of 1:1. 38 patients will receive Remogliflozin etabonate 100mg tablets, which they will have to consume twice daily orally for 42 days. Second group of 37 patients will be given Dapagliflozin 10mg which hey will have to consume once daily orally for 42 days. A CGM device will be CGM implanted with proper care at Baseline. To ensure a run-in period with existing recording of at least 3 days before randomization, patients would be asked to return to site after 4 days (visit 2). The patients would be provided with uniform meal plan to be followed during study period. The patient would be provided with diary to record the meals during period of CGM assessment. On visit 2, the patient would be randomized to any of the treatment arms as per randomization plan. The study drugs as per treatment allocation would be dispensed to the patients. A blood assessment of FPG & PPG would be performed. The meal diary would be assessed for any deviations in meal plan. The patients would be asked to follow-up after 7 to 8 days (completion of 14 days from the day of implantation of device). On follow-up day (visit 3), the CGM device would be removed and data shall be captured. The meal diary would be assessed for any deviations in meal plan & any adverse events since treatment initiation would be enquired. The therapy would be continued for period of 4 weeks post randomization & next follow-up would be planned 4 weeks after randomization. On Day 28 (visit 4), The meal diary would be assessed for any deviations in meal plan & any adverse events since treatment initiation would be enquired. A blood assessment of FPG & PPG would be performed. A CGM device would again be implanted & patient would be asked to follow-up 2 weeks later for CGM device. A blood assessment of FPG & PPG would be performed On day 42 (visit 5), all activities as on visit 3 would be performed. Additionally, blood collection would be performed for assessment as per schedule of assessments. Safety monitoring would be performed on continual basis over entire study duration for incident signs & symptoms. The patient would be asked to visit any time in case of adverse event. Any such visit would be considered as unscheduled visit.

trial_public_title: Efficacy of Oral Semaglutide in Overweight or Obesity
trial_scientific_title_: Efficacy of Oral Semaglutide in Overweight or Obesity : Randomized Controlled Trial (OSO Study - Oral Semaglutide for Obesity)
trial_intervention_name: DRUG: oral semaglutide
trial_co_investigators: Rakesh Kalapala, Anudeep Katrevula, Nitin Jagtap, Pratik Chhabra, Anand V Kulkarni
trial_description: All the eligible participants after screening will be randomized into two groups (Group 1 and 2) by block randomization (block size of 4 and 6) using computer generated random number. Randomization is performed by the individual not involved in the study. Participants in both groups will receive individual counselling sessions to help them adhere to a reduced-calorie diet (500-kcal deficit per day relative to the energy expenditure estimated at the time) and increased physical activity (with 150 minutes per week of physical activity, such as walking). Both diet and activity will be recorded daily in a diary or by use of a smartphone application or other tools and were reviewed during counselling sessions. Participants in group 1 also receive oral Semaglutide along with diet and exercise. Treatment will be initiated with the 3 mg once-daily dose. The dose will be increased to 7 mg and then maximum of 14mg per day in 2-4weeks interval based on patient tolerance. Dose escalation schedule will be used to decrease the gastrointestinal side effects. Participants will be assessed for gastrointestinal complaints (nausea, vomiting) during escalation and follow-up. Once the desired dose is achieved, patient will remain at the maximum 14mg daily dose unless a reduction was warranted owing to problems with side effects. In such cases, investigators will reescalate the dose once symptoms are resolved or diminished.The absorption of oral Semaglutide is affected by food and fluid in the stomach, hence patients receiving oral Semaglutide will be instructed to take each tablet with up to 120 mL of water in the morning, in a fasting state, and a minimum of 30 min before the first meal of the day. Counselling will be done by a dietician or a similar qualified healthcare professional every 4th week via visits/phone contacts till the completion of the study. Participants have to physically visit the hospital at the end of 28 weeks for assessment.

trial_public_title: Manipal Heart Failure Registry (MHFR)
trial_scientific_title_: Manipal Heart Failure Registry (MHFR): A Long Term, Prospective and Descriptive Study of Heart Failure in a Tertiary Care Hospital in South India
trial_indication: Heart Failure|Systolic Heart Failure|Diastolic Heart Failure|Decompensated Heart Failure|Acute Heart Failure
trial_description:
Burden of HF in India is approximate 2-5 million patients with an estimated prevalence of 2-3/1000 population. HF is responsible for approx1.8 million admissions annually in India. Very few studies are there in India which shows the detailed documentation of HF.

Manipal Heart failure Registry (MHFR) is an institutional level Heart Failure disease registry. In MHFR the investigator will document the detailed information about heart failure patients admitted or referred to Department of Cardiology, Kasturba Medical College, a constituent college and teaching hospital of Manipal University.

MHFR will include cohorts of patients with acute heart failure (AHF), with the intention of implementing a long-term follow-up, the use of health economic assessment to evaluate the degree of resource utilization; and quantifying the burden on quality of life for patients. Utilization of drug in HF patients will be evaluated as a part of this study to prevent the use of inappropriate drug therapy and to improve overall drug effectiveness and outcomes. Hence the protocol sought to identify the characteristics and long-term outcomes of Indian patients with acute heart failure admitted in an Indian tertiary care center in South India.

There are no risks to patients participating in the registry study, as standard of care quality will not be affected. It is unlikely that there will be any direct benefit as a result of participation in the HF-disease registry. However, the information contained within this non-interventional registry study will be used for research studies directed at improving the knowledge and treatment of acute heart failure as well as improving patients treatment in the future.

The potential impact of proposed research (Manipal Heart Failure Registry) is envisioned to be four-fold:

1. to enable a broad overview of the routine medical practices for HF treatment;
2. to assess the healthcare resource utilization and drug utilization review for heart failure patients
3. to assess the burden of disease (mortality, re-hospitalization) in the long term; and
4. to provide a novel overview of the impact of HF syndrome on quality of life as well as health economics.

trial_public_title: Use of risk scores to predict occurrence of bleeding events in patients undergoing heart blood vessel stenting
trial_scientific_title_: Use of risk scores to predict occurrence of bleeding events in patients undergoing percutaneous coronary intervention
trial_sponsors: Kasturba Medical College Manipal Academy of Higher Education Manipal
trial_indication: Kasturba Medical College Manipal Academy of Higher Education Manipal
trial_description:
1. Title of the Project: Use of risk scores to predict occurrence of bleeding events in patients undergoing percutaneous coronary intervention

2. Type of Study: Prospective, Observational, Single Centre Study

 

3. Aims & objectives:

 

Aims-

 

To assess the bleeding risk in patients undergoing Percutaneous Coronary Intervention (PCI)

 

Objectives-

 

To compare the ARC-HBR definition with Precise-DAPT bleeding risk score in predicting major bleeding events in patients undergoing PCI.
To Calculate the incidence of major bleeding events, ischemic events and mortality MACE in patients undergoing PCI at 6 months follow up.
 
4. Justification for study:
There are many Atrial Fibrillation score that have been developed to predict bleeding risk in patients of Atrial Fibrillation. But there are very few scores that have been validated to predict the bleeding events in patients undergoing percutaneous coronary intervention. Those scoring system have been developed to facilitate risk stratification of patients but effectiveness to identify patient at high bleeding risk remains. Recently proposed ARC-HBR consensus was done to portend an incidence of bleeding, but this study was done in western population. So, this study is carried out to assess the bleeding events in patients undergoing percutaneous coronary intervention in Indian population. To our knowledge, no such studies are done to predict the high bleeding risk in patients undergoing PCI using the proposed ARC-HBR criteria in India. This study will also compare ARC-HBR and PRECISE-DAPT score as these two scores are not compared previously in predicting major bleedings in patients undergoing PCI.

 

5. Departments Involved: Cardiology

 

6. Study Period: 1.5 year

 

7. Sample Size: 455 patients. Sample size was estimated at 5% level of significance with an allowable error of 5%, using the following formula: 

 n = Z2 spe(1-spe)/e2(1-prevalance)
z-5 1.96 at 95% C.I

A minimum sample size of 395 cases will be required for present study.

 

8. Materials and Methods: 

 A) Inclusion Criteria:

 1.All patients >18 years who undergo PCI with DES for standard medication at Kasturba Hospital, Manipal during the study period.

 B) Exclusion criteria:

 1.All patients who undergo PCI with bare metal stent and stent grafts.

 C) Biological materials required:

 None. Only reports from standard blood tests will be noted. No additional blood samples will be used for the purpose of the study.

 

 D) Statistical Methods:

 

 Descriptive statistical analysis will be carried out. Chi-square test and Fisher Exact test will be used whenever the data presented in terms of frequency, and student t-test will be used to test significance when the results are presented as mean Â± standard deviation (SD). Odds ratio (OR) and its confidence interval (CI) will be computed for event of interest. P value <0.05 will be considered statistically significant. The statistical software namely SPSS 22.0 will be used for the analysis of the data.
